perturbation.original_text: EXHIBIT A JOINT FILING AGREEMENT In accordance with Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned agree to the joint filing on behalf of each of them of a statement on Schedule 13G (including amendments thereto) with respect to the Common Stock of Spring Bank Pharmaceuticals, Inc. and further agree that this agreement be included as an exhibit to such filing.
perturbation.changed_text: EXHIBIT A JOINT FILING UNDERTAKING In accordance with Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned consent to the collaborative submission by each of them of a statement on Form 13G (including supplements thereto) regarding the Common Shares of Spring Bank Pharmaceuticals, Inc. and further acknowledge that this arrangement will be appended as a display to that submission.
perturbation.explanation: The change from "AGREEMENT" to "UNDERTAKING", "joint filing" to "collaborative submission", "Schedule 13G" to "Form 13G", and "Common Stock" to "Common Shares" introduces ambiguity. Although they may be referring to the same thing, the document never defines them as such. Specifically, the contradiction arises because later in the text, the document refers back to "this agreement," creating uncertainty as to whether the parties are still bound by the terms outlined under the new description as an "undertaking".
perturbation.contradicted_location: Later sentences in the beginning section
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party to this agreement expressly authorizes each other party to file on its behalf any and all amendments to such statement. Each party to this agreement agrees that this joint filing agreement may be signed in counterparts.
perturbation.justification: The changed text uses different terminology ("UNDERTAKING" instead of "AGREEMENT", "Form 13G" instead of "Schedule 13G", etc.) to describe the same filing arrangement. This creates ambiguity regarding which document and terms the parties are bound to. The contradicted text refers to "this agreement." Because the changed text renames the "agreement" as an "undertaking" and a "collaborative submission on Form 13G", it directly creates a conflict, as it's unclear if the parties are still bound by the terms referring to the original "agreement." This ambiguity leads to a strong contradiction regarding the obligations and terms.

perturbation.original_text: Each party to the agreement expressly authorizes each other party to file on its behalf any and all amendments to such statement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each signatory to the accord explicitly empowers every other signatory to present on its account any and all revisions to that declaration.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "party to the agreement" to "signatory to the accord" and "amendments to such statement" to "revisions to that declaration" introduces terminology that is not defined within the document. This creates uncertainty, particularly because it's not clarified whether a 'signatory to the accord' is the same as a 'party to the agreement' or if 'declaration' and 'statement' have the same meaning. This becomes problematic because it creates an ambiguity on if both entities are bound by the original agreement
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party to this agreement agrees that this joint filing agreement may be signed in counterparts.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces "party to the agreement" with "signatory to the accord" and "amendments to such statement" with "revisions to that declaration." The explanation states these new terms are undefined, creating ambiguity regarding whether a 'signatory to the accord' is the same as a 'party to the agreement' and if 'declaration' and 'statement' have the same meaning. The contradicted text references "Each party to this agreement agrees that this joint filing agreement may be signed in counterparts." If a 'signatory' is not necessarily a 'party', then the ability to sign in counterparts is called into question for signatories. This constitutes a strong contradiction because the core identity of who is bound by the agreement is made unclear, therefore creating ambiguity and a potential issue with enforceability of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: In evidence whereof, the undersigned have caused this Agreement to be executed on their behalf this 7t h day of April, 2020.
perturbation.changed_text: Testifying to which, the individuals below have permitted this Arrangement to be operational on their authority this 7th day of April, 2020.
perturbation.explanation: Switching from "Agreement to be executed" to "Arrangement to be operational" presents an internal contradiction, especially since earlier the document was renamed from "AGREEMENT" to "UNDERTAKING". This raises questions if "Arrangement" is synonymous to "Agreement" or "Undertaking". This uncertainty creates confusion regarding when the agreement officially begins.
perturbation.contradicted_text: UBS ONCOLOGY IMPACT FUND L.P. ONCOLOGY IMPACT FUND (CAYMAN) MANAGEMENT L.P. By: Oncology Impact Fund (Cayman) Management L.P, By: MPM Oncology Impact Management LP, its General Partner its General Partner By: MPM Oncology Impact Management LP, By: MPM Oncology Impact Management GP LLC, its General Partner its General Partner By: MPM Oncology Impact Management GP LLC, By: /s/ Ansbert Gadicke its General Partner Name: Ansbert Gadicke Title: Managing Member By: /s/ Ansbert Gadicke Name: Ansbert Gadicke Title: Managing Member MPM ONCOLOGY IMPACT MANAGEMENT LP MPM ONCOLOGY IMPACT MANAGEMENT GP LLC By: MPM Oncology Impact Management GP LLC, By: /s/ Ansbert Gadicke its General Partner Name: Ansbert Gadicke Title: Managing Member By: /s/ Ansbert Gadicke Name: Ansbert Gadicke Title: Managing Member
perturbation.justification: The change from "Agreement to be executed" to "Arrangement to be operational" introduces misaligned terminology. The contradicted text references specific entities operating under the "UBS ONCOLOGY IMPACT FUND L.P. ONCOLOGY IMPACT FUND (CAYMAN) MANAGEMENT L.P." agreement. The change introduces uncertainty by using 'Arrangement' instead of 'Agreement,' and 'operational' instead of 'executed.' This creates ambiguity about whether the contradicted text is still relevant to the modified text, potentially leading to disputes over the validity and enforceability of the terms for those entities. The explanation states this contradiction clearly, further confirming the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' as valid.
